subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: The capacity of a settling tank must be authorised by the Permit Authority.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: The settling tank and baffles are to be constructed of a minimum—

ol.li: other authorised acid and alkali resistant material.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: The diameter of the outlet pipe must be—

ol.li: DN 100 when the outlet size is DN 100; or
a minimum one size larger than the inlet pipe.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A settling tank must be located in a position that enables ready access for maintenance, sampling and inspection.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: Where a settling tank is to be located below ground level, it must—

ol.li: be surrounded with concrete walls and base with a minimum thickness of 100 mm; and
omit the 25 mm sampling valve; and
have a minimum DN 100 outlet pipe located no deeper than 600 mm below surface level; and
have the outlet pipe connected to a disconnector gully fitted with a loos fitting grate or screwed cap.

callout.p: Where the Permit Authority has not specified the capacity of a settling tank in accordance with C4D19(1), it must be—

ol.li: equivalent to the maximum hourly discharge; and
no less than 72 litres, below the outlet.

callout.p: See TAS Figure C4D19 for a typical settling tank.
